purpose: Given a set of PREREQ_GAP events, build a dependency graph, determine execution order (OVERLAP_DETECTED before MISSING_FABRIC_INTERFACE), classify each as autonomously resolvable or requiring human decision, and dispatch in order.

n1.description: Retrieve prerequisite-resolution-protocol pattern and architecture decisions for overlap context

n2.description: Build dependency graph from prereqGaps. Rules: OVERLAP_DECISION gaps block MISSING_FABRIC_INTERFACE gaps that reference the same service category. MACHINE_CONSTANT gaps are independent. Topological sort produces execution order.

rules: OVERLAP_DETECTED must resolve before MISSING_FABRIC_INTERFACE in same category
MACHINE_CONSTANT for a service that also has MISSING_FABRIC_INTERFACE is DEPENDENT on the interface gap
MACHINE_CONSTANT with no corresponding MISSING_FABRIC_INTERFACE is independent — can resolve in parallel
autonomouslyResolvable=false goes to human escalation queue

n3.description: Branch: if humanEscalations exist → emit to FLOW-PREREQ-03. Then dispatch autonomous gaps to FLOW-PREREQ-02.

n4-escalate-then-dispatch.description: Emit overlap decisions to FLOW-PREREQ-03, then dispatch remaining autonomous gaps. The escalated gaps block downstream gaps in the same category.

n5-dispatch-autonomous.description: All gaps are autonomously resolvable. Dispatch to FLOW-PREREQ-02 in dependency order.

ironRules: Per CF-789: ordering must complete before any FLOW-PREREQ-02 execution begins
Per CF-790: every gap in input must appear in output — none suppressed, none added
CF-791: OVERLAP_DETECTED gaps MUST resolve before MISSING_FABRIC_INTERFACE gaps in the same service category — topological ordering is mandatory
autonomouslyResolvable=false gaps MUST route to FLOW-PREREQ-03 human escalation — never auto-resolve architectural decisions
MACHINE_CONSTANT gaps for a service that also has MISSING_FABRIC_INTERFACE are DEPENDENT — interface must resolve first, then constant registration
qualityGates: Every gap in input appears exactly once in output (executionOrder + humanEscalations)
No MISSING_FABRIC_INTERFACE gap precedes an OVERLAP_DETECTED gap in the same category
